key_points: Farming is the main production activity in Palampur, supported by non-farm activities like dairy, transport, and small-scale manufacturing.
The four requirements (factors) for production are Land, Labour, Physical Capital, and Human Capital.
Physical Capital is divided into Fixed Capital (tools, machines, buildings) and Working Capital (raw materials, money in hand).
Land is a fixed resource; production can be increased through Multiple Cropping (growing more than one crop on the same piece of land) and modern farming methods.
The Green Revolution in the late 1960s introduced HYV (High Yielding Variety) seeds, chemical fertilizers, and pesticides to increase wheat and rice yields.
Modern farming methods require more capital and have led to environmental issues like soil fertility loss and groundwater depletion.
Labor is provided either by the farm family (small farmers) or hired workers (medium and large farmers).
Capital is arranged by small farmers through high-interest borrowing, while medium/large farmers use their own savings from surplus production.
formulas: Yield: Total Crop Production / Area of Land
Multiple Cropping: Growing >1 crop on the same land per year
Factors of Production: Land + Labour + Physical Capital + Human Capital
examples: Fixed Capital in Palampur: Tractors, electric tubewells, wooden ploughs, and sugarcane crushing machines.
Working Capital: Raw materials like yarn for weavers, clay for potters, and cash to pay wages or buy seeds.
Multiple Cropping: Growing Jowar and Bajra (Kharif), Potato (Third crop), and Wheat (Rabi) on the same plot.
Non-Farm Activities: Dairy farming (selling milk in Raiganj), small general stores, and rickshaw/tractor transport services.
Small Scale Manufacturing: Mishrilal using a mechanical sugarcane crusher to make jaggery (gur).
exam_tips: Differentiate clearly between Fixed and Working capital; remember that Fixed capital lasts for years.
Focus on the 'Three Ways to Increase Production': Irrigation, Multiple Cropping, and Modern Inputs.
Understand the impact of the Green Revolution—it increased food production but harmed the environment.
In labor-related questions, remember that farm laborers in Palampur often earn less than the minimum wage due to heavy competition.
Identify Raiganj (large village) and Shahpur (small town) as the key market links for Palampur.
